cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out between what times Jennifer could have gone to the swimming pool. The key here is to look at the timeline of her activities and see when she was free. Let me list out all the given information step by step. First, Jennifer woke up at 5am. Then Richard saw her working at the office from 5am to 8am. So from 5 to 8, she was at the office. Next, Samantha saw her reading at the library from 12pm to 1pm. Then Betty saw her at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. Sarah saw her buying a phone from 2pm to 9pm. Thomas saw her walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. And the swimming pool is closed after 10pm. So the question is asking for the time frame when Jennifer could have gone to the pool. The pool is open until 10pm, so she can't go after that. But she also can't be at the pool during the times she was seen elsewhere. Let me map out her schedule: - 5am to 8am: Office - 8am to 12pm: Unaccounted for - 12pm to 1pm: Library - 1pm to 2pm: Bike shop - 2pm to 9pm: Electronics store - 9pm to 10pm: Walking to Statue of Liberty - 10pm onwards: Pool closed So the only time when she wasn't doing any of these activities is between 8am and 12pm. Because after 8am until 12pm, there are no reports of her activities. Let me check again: From 5am to 8am: office. Then from 8am, nothing until 12pm. Then from 12pm to 1pm: library. So between 8am and 12pm, she's free. So she could have gone to the pool during that time. The pool is open until 10pm, so she could have gone as late as 10pm, but she was seen at the electronics store from 2pm to 9pm and then walking to the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. Therefore, the only possible time she could have gone to the pool is between 8am and 12pm. Looking at the options, option A is 8am to 12pm. The other options conflict with her known activities.
